title: erycibe coriacea
intro: Erycibe coriacea, also known as the "bent-back vine," is a type of plant native to the tropical regions of Asia and Australia, belonging to the family Polygalaceae. Erycibe coriacea has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Erycibe coriacea include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and provide antioxidant protection against oxidative stress and cell damage. Erycibe coriacea is often prepared as a decoction or infusion for its medicinal uses, and it can also be found in some herbal supplements in capsule or powder form.
